en.title: Grammar Contrast Check
en.prompt: Could you please help me to ensure that the grammar and spelling are correct? Do not tryto improve the text, if no mistake is found, tellme that this paragraph is good.lf you find grammar or spelling mistakes, please list the mistakes you find in a two-column markdown table, put the original text in the first column.put the corrected text in the second columnand do highlight the key words you fixed in bold

related.title: High schoolers' essay
related.remark: The article produced by this prompt reflects the writing style of Chinese middle school students. Contributed by @Qizhen-Yang.

related.title: Four-layered Structure Induction
related.remark: Multi-level summarization and induction can be used to explain words and phrases and make associations with the article. The Chinese version of this prompt has better effect. Contributed by @ergf991.

metaDescription: Text errors? Identify grammar and spelling mistakes without changing style. Get a clear comparison table of fixes. Try it now.

faq.q: What's the advantage of the two-column table comparison?
faq.a: Far more intuitive than "a paragraph pointing out errors." You see "original vs. corrected" differences at a glance, fitting English learners who archive mistakes. Save the table to Notion as a personal error library; long-term review shows clear progress.

faq.q: Will the AI accidentally "improve" rather than "correct"?
faq.a: The prompt stresses "don't improve, only correct," but the model occasionally goes beyond instruction to tweak expression. For changes beyond grammar (like swapping a synonym), keep the original yourself. For strict mode, append "if there's no grammar error, return original, no substitutions allowed."
